data.title: Burwick Law: No connection with Dogshit2, no Meme coins launched on the chain
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Max Burwick, managing partner of Burwick Law, the law firm currently suing pump.fun, declined to comment on who first launched the Meme coin Dogshit2 and questioned the importance of the issue.
Dogshit2 appears in Burwick Law's evidence file against pump.fun. The file shows the gradual creation of the token, which seems to indicate that it was launched by the company during the prosecution of pump.fun.
However, Burwick Law said, "Our company has no association, recognition or ownership interest in the Dogshit2 token or any related assets. In short, our company did not launch any Meme coins on the chain."
After being asked to comment on Dogshit2, Burwick added, "I don’t understand the interest."
When asked if he or anyone associated with Burwick Law entered the details of Dogshit2 and clicked "Create Token". Burwick responded, “I’m confused, why does this matter?” and added, “Why is it so important?”
Burwick then questioned the legitimacy of the question and asked if people were concluding that victims who have been taken advantage of and harmed should not be allowed legal representation. (Protos)

data.title: European leaders: Talks on Russia-Ukraine conflict without Europe 'unacceptable'
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Regarding the US's "unilateral" negotiations with Russia on the Russia-Ukraine conflict, leaders of many European countries recently stated during the Munich Security Conference (MSC) that negotiations without the participation of European countries and Ukraine are "unacceptable".
Merz, chairman of the Christian Democratic Union of Germany, said at the MSC that Europe must participate in negotiations to end the Ukrainian war, "Russia and the United States are negotiating without the participation of Ukraine and European countries, which is absolutely unacceptable."
Finnish President Stubb told reporters during the MSC: "Without Europeans, we cannot discuss or negotiate about the future of Ukraine or the European security structure. But Europe needs to unite and do more with less talk." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Coinbase Pro Bitcoin wallet balance increased by nearly 64,000 in the past 30 days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinglass data shows that the current Coinbase Pro Bitcoin wallet balance is 771,503.63, ranking first among CEX; 508.37 inflows in the past 24 hours, 454.35 inflows in the past 7 days, and 63955.61 inflows in the past 30 days.
The balance of Binance Bitcoin wallet is 566,442.96, with an outflow of 169.71 in the past 24 hours, 225.32 outflows in the past 7 days, and 346.51 inflows in the past 30 days.
The balance of Bitfinex Bitcoin wallet is 363,835.78, with an outflow of 440.20 in the past 24 hours, an inflow of 7128.64 in the past 7 days, and an outflow of 514.47 in the past 30 days.

data.title: An insider bought 13.73 million LIBRA and then sold them for a profit of nearly 10 million US dollars
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, yesterday, an insider of LIBRA spent $2.8 million USDC to buy 13.73 million LIBRA through two different wallets.
Then, the insider sent these LIBRA to multiple wallets and sold them for $12.79 million USDC, and finally made a profit of $9.99 million.

data.title: Startale CEO Calls For Addressing Meme Coin Dominance In Crypto Industry
data.description: According to Odaily, Startale CEO Sota Watanabe recently expressed concerns on X about the current state of the cryptocurrency industry. Reflecting on his decade-long journey in the field, Watanabe shared his initial excitement about how technology could empower individuals and improve society. However, he noted that the industry's primary use case has shifted to meme coins. While acknowledging this reality, Watanabe emphasized the need to address this issue.

data.title: BNB Chain Hits $15 Million in Gas Fees Over 7 Days, Second Only to Solana
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from Nansen reveals that BNB Chain's gas fees have accumulated to $15 million over the past seven days, marking a 400% increase compared to the previous period. This figure is second only to Solana, which recorded $29 million in gas fees. In a recent update, validators on the BNB Chain will now be able to collect more bids within the same block. This change could potentially allow high-value transactions, such as time-sensitive MEME trades, to replace lower-priority transactions.

data.title: BNB Chain launches three MEV protection solutions, CZ comments: one step at a time
Traditional Companies Develop Crypto Applications on Ethereum
data.description:
According to PANews, a report by Christine Kim, Vice President of Research at Galaxy Digital, reveals that over 50 traditional companies, including financial institutions like Deutsche Bank and PayPal, as well as brands such as Louis Vuitton and Adidas, are developing cryptocurrency-specific applications on Ethereum and its Layer 2 networks. These initiatives focus on non-speculative use cases, such as the tokenization of real-world assets (RWA), non-fungible tokens (NFTs), and Web3 gaming, bypassing general crypto infrastructure like exchanges.

Ethereum leads in RWA tokenization, with assets under custody valued at nearly ten times that of its competitor blockchain, Stellar. Among the 20 financial institutions building crypto infrastructure, 13 have issued RWAs, including Blackrock's Ethereum-based fund BUIDL. Stablecoins are also thriving on Ethereum, with PayPal's PYUSD and Robinhood's USDG contributing to a 70% surge in supply in 2024. Ethereum holds over 50% of the $400 billion stablecoin market.

data.title: India Seizes $190 Million in Cryptocurrency Linked to Bitconnect Scheme
data.description:
According to Odaily, Indian authorities have seized nearly $190 million in cryptocurrency connected to the Bitconnect Ponzi scheme, which was dismantled in 2018. The scheme resulted in losses of approximately $2.4 billion for 4,000 investors across 95 countries. Bitconnect, launched in 2016, collapsed in 2018. The founder, Satish Kumbhani, was charged by the U.S. Department of Justice in February 2022 for establishing a global network of promoters who were paid commissions to promote the scheme.

In August of last year, the Indian Enforcement Directorate (ED) reported the arrest of Shailesh Babulal Bhatt from Gujarat. Bhatt was accused of kidnapping, extortion, and violating anti-money laundering laws in a major cryptocurrency-related crime exceeding $144 million. Following a complaint filed by Surat police against Bitconnect Coin promoter Satish Kumbhani, the Indian Ministry of Justice launched a money laundering investigation, leading to Bhatt's detention.

It is alleged that after Kumbhani shut down the Bitconnect platform in January 2018, he deceived investors and fled. Bhatt, who had invested in Bitconnect, kidnapped two of Kumbhani's employees to recover his funds, extorting 2,091 Bitcoins, 11,000 Litecoins, and cash amounting to 145 million rupees, now valued at 12.325 billion rupees. Bhatt also distributed 2.89 billion rupees of the extorted funds among accomplices for acquiring various assets.

data.title: Indian Authorities Seize $190 Million in Cryptocurrency Linked to Crypto Ponzi Scheme BitConnect
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Indian authorities have seized nearly $190 million in cryptocurrencies related to Bitconnect in their ongoing investigation into a crypto Ponzi scheme that was uncovered in 2018 and caused 4,000 investors in 95 countries to lose about $2.4 billion. Bitconnect was launched in 2016 and closed in 2018.
It is understood that Bitconnect founder Satish Kumbhani (charged by the US Department of Justice in February 2022) established a global network of promoters to pay them commissions to promote the Ponzi scheme. (Cointelegraph)
Last August, the Indian Enforcement Directorate (ED) said that a man in Gujarat, Shailesh Babulal Bhatt, was arrested on suspicion of kidnapping and extortion and violating anti-money laundering laws. The incident involved a major cryptocurrency-related crime of more than $144 million.
Bhatt was detained following a money laundering investigation launched by the Indian Ministry of Justice based on the first filing by the Surat police against Bitconnect Coin promoter Satish Kumbhani. Kumbhani allegedly defrauded investors and fled after shutting down the Bitconnect platform in January 2018.
Bhatt had invested in Bitconnect and in order to recover the funds, he kidnapped two of Kumbhani’s employees and extorted 2,091 Bitcoins, 11,000 Litecoins and Rs 145 million in cash, which is now worth Rs 12.325 billion. Bhatt also distributed Rs 289 million of the extortion funds to his accomplices for the acquisition of various assets.
